text:
Our CEO @jerryjliu0 in @VentureBeat, on what's actually changing in the LLM stack:

"We've really identified that there's a core set of data that has been locked up in all these file format containers. Ultimately, whether you use OpenAI Codex or Claude Code doesn't really
media: link_card: venturebeat.com, The scaffolding era is over. LlamaIndex says context is the new moat
notes: Post is partially hidden behind X show-more. Visible link card states the main thesis: context is the new moat.

text:
Thank you AI Dev Day '26 @DeepLearningAI

@jerryjliu0 shares why SOTA LLMs can build an app but can't read a PDF
notes: Event framing makes PDF/document reading the gap left by otherwise strong coding agents.

text:
Building scalable, distributed document processing pipelines isn't easy.
That's why we teamed up with @render to build a system that:

Leverages the LlamaParse platform to parse, classify, extract, and retrieve information from documents

Uses Render Workflows to distribute
notes: High-bookmark product workflow post; strong signal for production document pipeline architecture.

text:
We shipped a LlamaParse MCP server to let you parse, classify, split, and generally operate over your hardest documents with your favorite AI agent

Check out the MCP server: mcp.llamaindex.ai/mcp

This is both a useful feature and a great piece of engineering that we want
notes: CEO amplification of MCP server; strong signal that LlamaParse is being packaged as an agent-compatible document operation layer.

text:
Parsing documents with AI agents just got a lot more seamless

We've rebuilt the LlamaParse MCP server to handle your document processing workflows, and you can connect it today to any MCP-compatible client at mcp.llamaindex.ai/mcp

Once connected, you'll be able to:
notes: Original product announcement for rebuilt LlamaParse MCP server.

text:
Let's talk document formatting.

Bold. Italics. Superscripts. Strikethroughs. The visual cues humans rely on every time we read a doc, and ones existing OCR benchmarks completely ignore.

"$199" struck through next to "$149" isn't decoration. It's the meaning.
A superscript
notes: Explains why document OCR needs layout and formatting semantics, not just text extraction.
